---
title: "Introduction"
---
::: {.callout-tip collapse=true}
# Learning Goals: Introduction
**Course**

1. Know who your professor is  
2. Know how I will assess your mastery of course content

**Scientific Foundations**

1. Recognize that there are multiple scientifically valid ways to study human language; identify scientific evidence relevant to understanding (i) how languages are organized structurally, (ii) how humans perceive and process language in real time, and (iii) the brain basis of language
2. Define *localization*
3. Summarize the classical model of language in the brain and describe the original evidence for that model
4. Define *lateralization*
5. Describe two methods that can be used to assess lateralization
5. Explain the difference between *functional specialization* and *functional specificity*
6. Summarize the approach of 19th-century phrenology, identify unscientific aspects of this approach, and give an example of the societal harm it caused 

**Brain Geography**

1. Be able to roughly locate Broca's area and Wernicke's area on a side view of the brain
2. Identify and distinguish *coronal*, *axial*, and *sagittal* views of the human brain; for each view, be able to tell which way the brain is "facing"
3. "Navigate" to a region of the brain using the following neuroanatomical and directional terms: *frontal*, *temporal*, *parietal*, *occipital*, *anterior*, *posterior*, *inferior*, *superior*, *lateral*, *medial*, *sulcus*, *gyrus*, *left*, *right*

:::

| Date | Class Topic | Slides | Due |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Thurs 8/27 | [Syllabus](index.qmd), [assignments](requirements.qmd), [schedule](schedule.md) | [↗](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Sfffbc1-twq_R9KZWHKwUxCgpskvh7g66OaMdo1Z-OYOKt820/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p) |
| Tues 9/1 | Studying language; types of evidence | [↗](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit?slide=id.g3d40f98fbaf_3_0#slide=id.g3d40f98fbaf_3_0)| <span class="reading-badge">SGN Ch. 1, *Introduction* (p. 1-9)</span>
| Thurs 9/3 | Localization, classical model | [↗](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/17lZzHZe0HFpVxne9vD_WBngnSxhRwL6ujtAinhx3ULc/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p) | <span class="reading-badge">SGN Ch. 2, *The Toolbox* (p. 10-17)</span> 
| Tues 9/8 | *No class (Monday schedule)* | | 
| Thurs 9/10 | Lateralization | [↗](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1AjYvk7ckzUOj0pFkItxFlR4eAeioK25fLUiPUwgR_9k/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p)
| Monday 9/14| | |<span class="controversy-badge">Choose issue</span>|
| Tues 9/15 | Brain geography | [↗](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1OF3vQvqHdez7yOyLC89PxaUPbzD0EITDRsRl_dkVMsc/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p)| <span class="reading-badge">SGN Ch. 2, *The Toolbox* (p. 18-25)</span>
| Thurs 9/17 | <span class="exam-badge">Exam #1</span> |



**Resources**

* [Allen Brain Reference Atlases](https://atlas.brain-map.org/)
* [Neurotorium Brain Atlas](https://neurotorium.org/tool/brain-atlas/)
* [UBC: The Cortex](https://www.neuroanatomy.ca/modules/Cortex/story.html)
* [Dichotic listening task](https://learninglink.oup.com/access/content/sedivy-2e-student-resources/sedivy2e-chapter-3-web-activity-1?previousFilter=tag_chapter-03)
